message:
feat(recording): Cx-first _with_cx suite on RecordingManager (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Adds four Cx-first `_with_cx` variants on `RecordingManager`,
mirroring the cass.rs tick 11-12 pattern and leveraging the
`Mutex::lock_with_cx` primitive from tick 9. Downstream modules
that have a `&Cx` in hand can now drive the recording manager's
active-recorder lifecycle without going through ambient
`cx::for_request()` indirection on the internal mutex.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs (+145 src, +77 tests)

   New Cx-first public entry points (all asupersync-runtime gated):

     * `start_recording_with_cx(cx, pane_id, path, started_at_ms)`
     * `stop_recording_with_cx(cx, pane_id)`
     * `record_segment_with_cx(cx, segment)`
     * `record_event_with_cx(cx, pane_id, detection, captured_at_ms)`

   Each uses `self.recorders.lock_with_cx(cx).await` instead of
   `self.recorders.lock().await`, so a caller-cancelled wait is
   bound directly to the caller's Cx rather than being pulled from
   `Cx::current()` thread-local state.

   Legacy methods under `asupersync-runtime` delegate to the
   `_with_cx` variants via `cx::for_request()`. Non-asupersync
   fallback preserves the inline `.lock()` pattern.

2. Two new tests:

   * `recording_manager_with_cx_full_cycle_matches_legacy`:
     start_recording_with_cx → record_segment_with_cx →
     stop_recording_with_cx end-to-end. Asserts stats.bytes_raw
     reflects the recorded content (no-regression pin on full
     Cx-first path).

   * `recording_manager_stop_with_cx_nonexistent_returns_none`:
     pins the None contract on unknown pane_id under the Cx-first
     entry point.

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib recording::: 91/91 pass
    (89 existing + 2 new Cx-first)
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress. RecordingManager is now the
fourth multi-method surface to gain a complete Cx-first suite (after
CautClient, ProtocolRecoveryEngine, CassClient).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
